tools.description:
Searches the local project codebase and documentation using semantic similarity.
Call this tool whenever the user asks about: how code works, where something is
implemented, project structure, functions, modules, configuration, or any
project-specific question. Returns relevant code snippets and docs with file paths.
tools.name: rag_search

tools.description: Fetch full content of specific chunks by IDs returned from rag_search.
tools.name: rag_get_chunks

tools.description: Read specific line ranges or symbol definitions from a file.
tools.name: rag_get_file_ranges

tools.description: Return a token-efficient skeleton of a file: signatures, struct/enum/type definitions, imports and doc comments, with function bodies elided to '...'. Prefer this over reading whole files when you only need to understand a file's structure.
tools.name: rag_get_skeleton

tools.description: Find where a symbol (function, class, struct, etc.) is defined. Returns file path, line number, and call graph edges.
tools.name: nav_symbol_resolve

tools.description: Return the call graph around a symbol: what it calls and what calls it.
tools.name: nav_call_graph

tools.description: Given symbols or file paths, return which files and symbols would be affected by changes. Use before refactoring.
tools.name: impact_analyze

tools.description: Single call to get a complete, token-budgeted context package for a task. Combines semantic search results, relevant symbols, impact summary, and a project tree snapshot. Call this FIRST before any other tool.
tools.name: context_bundle

tools.description: Returns RAG index statistics: files indexed, chunks, last commit, dirty file count.
tools.name: rag_index_status

tools.description: Incrementally re-indexes all changed files. Call this to make sure the index is current before starting a task.
tools.name: rag_ensure_index

tools.description: Semantic diff of code changes: which SYMBOLS changed (added / removed / signature_changed / modified) and their blast radius — callers (from the symbol graph) and dependent files (from the import graph). Use this for PR/commit review and to write accurate commit messages (e.g. "changed the return type of X, which affects Y and Z"). Defaults to the working tree vs HEAD; pass a ref like "HEAD~1" or a range like "main..HEAD".

ref.description: Git ref or range (default: working tree vs HEAD)

tools.name: review_semantic_diff
